sutta: now a certain monk visited the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 lord they say living by dhamma living by dhamma lord how does a monk live by dhamma just stop here just to say this word lord actually is a translation of the word during the buddha's time the monks call each other abuso reverend or friend and only the buddha was called the bandain but later when the buddha was about to pass into mahaparinibana he instructed the junior monks to call the senior monks bante and that the senior monks could call the junior monks abuso reverend or fran or just by their name then the buddha replied monk consider the monk who masters dhamma he spends the day in that mastery he neglects to go apart and devotes not himself to tranquilization of the mind chaitosamata that monk is said to be swift to master but he lives not by dhamma consider the monk who teaches others dhamma in detail as he has heard it as he has mastered it he spends the day in convincing others of dhamma he neglects to go apart and devotes not himself to tranquilization of the mind monk that monk is said to be swift to convince but he lives not by dhamma again consider the monk who gives in full a repetition of dhamma as he has heard it as he has learned it he spends the day in repeating it he neglects to go apart and devotes not himself to tranquilization of the mind [Music] monk that monk is said to be swift to repeat but he lives not by dhamma then consider the monk who turns his mind to dhamma ponders over it reflects on it as he has heard it as he has learned it [Music] he spends his day in thinking about dhamma he neglects to go apart and devotes not himself to tranquilization of the mind monk that monk is said to be swift to think but he lives not by dhamma but monk take the case of the monk who masters dhamma and spends not the day in that mastery neglects not to go apart and devote himself to tranquilization of the mind verily monk such a monk is 1 who lives by dhamma mang thus barely have i declared 1 swift to master 1 swift to convince 1 swift to repeat 1 swift to think and 1 who lives by dhamma monk what should be done by a teacher for his disciples seeking their good from compassion and out of pity that has been done by me for you behold these 3 roots these empty places among cultivate jana monk and be not slothful reproach not yourself afterwards this is our command to you that's the end of the suta
commentary: here the buddha is saying that studying the sutas teaching repeating and reflecting on the sutas or dhamma are important but if 1 if 1 does not practice chaitosamata tranquilization of the mind 1 does not live by dhamma that means although the monk does the other duties he must devote himself sometime to tranquilization of the mind this word comes from the word the is tranquilization so you find that in the sutas samata practice is very much a part of the buddhist meditation and in addition to vipassana but if you look into the sutas more often than not now the word samatha is stressed more than vipassana because samatha tranquilization of the mind is the partner where we get rid of the hindrances and you have heard the sutas before that the buddha said that the hindrances is that which prevents us from getting inside so we have to cultivate some mata to get the inside and both samatha and vipassana are part and parcel of buddhist meditation um you've also heard previously 1 of the sutas and that buddha said that when we attain the samadhi the hindrances are restrained without a conscious effort and then 1 gets to see things as they really are and the last part of the suta the buddha said the tories monks to cultivate jhana the word here is [Music] and it means to cultivate jhana so [Music] sometimes translators translate this as meditate but the literal meaning is to cultivate jhana that is why when buddhism went to china the meditation school was called the chan the chan school and that word chan comes from the word chan no and chan no is actually from the word jhana so in the early days meditation was synonymous with cultivating jhana that's why the buddha here says to told his monks to cultivate jhana and this is quite an important suture because a monk can get caught up with many duties and activities and that is 1 of the hindrances to the holy life you see later there are others like talking sleeping etc so we are seeing that manga can get caught up with many duties and activities and that is 1 of the hindrances to the holy life and so a good monastery and should have minimum hours of group sitting for everyone the next is
